title: Erdős conjecture on arithmetic progressions
statement: If A ⊆ ℕ and the sum of reciprocals of A diverges, then A contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.

unknowns.description: a proof (or refutation) of the divergence⇒long-APs implication

title: Szemerédi's theorem
statement: Every subset of ℕ with positive upper density contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.

unknowns.description: a proof of the density⇒long-APs statement

title: Green–Tao theorem
statement: The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.

unknowns.description: a proof of long APs in the primes

title: Furstenberg multiple recurrence
statement: Every measure-preserving system exhibits multiple recurrence — the ergodic-theory statement equivalent to Szemerédi's theorem via the correspondence principle.

unknowns.description: a proof of multiple recurrence

refs: equivalent to Szemerédi via the correspondence principle

title: Erdős discrepancy problem
statement: For any ±1 sequence, the discrepancy over homogeneous arithmetic progressions is unbounded.

unknowns.description: a proof of unbounded discrepancy

title: Erdős–Szekeres convex polygon conjecture
statement: Any set of 2^(n-2)+1 points in general position contains n points in convex position (the exact bound).

unknowns.description: a proof of the exact 2^(n-2)+1 bound

criteria.test: an accepted proof of the exact bound

refs: asymptotics resolved by Suk 2016; exact bound open

title: Erdős–Hajnal conjecture
statement: For every graph H there is c(H)>0 such that any H-free graph on n vertices has a clique or independent set of size n^c(H).

unknowns.description: a proof for all H

refs: open in general; many special cases known

title: Growth rate of diagonal Ramsey numbers
statement: Determine the exponential growth rate of R(k,k) — the base of the exponent between √2 and 4.

unknowns.description: the growth constant

refs: upper bound improved (Campos–Griffiths–Morris–Sahasrabudhe 2023); exact base open

title: Erdős–Faber–Lovász conjecture
statement: The chromatic index of any linear hypergraph on n vertices is at most n.

unknowns.description: a proof for all n

title: Erdős distinct distances problem
statement: N points in the plane determine at least ~N/√(log N) distinct distances (sharp exponent).

unknowns.description: a proof of the near-linear lower bound

criteria.test: an accepted proof of the sharp exponent

title: Erdős unit distances problem
statement: The maximum number of unit distances among N points in the plane is N^(1+O(1/loglog N)) (Erdős's conjectured near-linear bound).

unknowns.description: a proof of the conjectured bound, or a construction refuting it

criteria.test: an accepted proof of the conjectured bound
exhibit a point configuration (counterexample construction) beating the conjectured bound

refs: status here is the pre-2026 snapshot; closed by an event — see events.json

title: Erdős–Ko–Rado theorem
statement: A maximal intersecting family of k-subsets of an n-set (n≥2k) has size C(n-1,k-1).

unknowns.description: a proof of the extremal bound

title: Erdős–Straus conjecture
statement: For every n≥2, 4/n = 1/a + 1/b + 1/c has a solution in positive integers.

unknowns.description: a proof for all n

title: Erdős–Rényi giant component threshold
statement: In G(n,p) a giant component emerges sharply at the threshold p = 1/n.

unknowns.description: a proof of the phase transition

title: Erdős–Turán conjecture on additive bases
statement: If A is an additive basis of order 2 then the representation function r_A(n) is unbounded.

unknowns.description: a proof of unboundedness

title: Erdős–Gyárfás conjecture
statement: Every graph of minimum degree 3 contains a cycle whose length is a power of 2.

unknowns.description: a proof for all such graphs

title: Structure vs. randomness (grand dichotomy)
statement: Grand meta-problem: every sufficiently large or dense object decomposes into a structured part plus a pseudorandom part, and structure forces prescribed patterns. (An organizing conjecture, not a single formal statement.)

unknowns.description: a unifying framework making the dichotomy precise across settings

criteria.test: a framework accepted as unifying the named instances

refs: organizing theme (Tao); inserted as an explicit top node for the demo

title: Roth's theorem (3-term APs)
statement: Every subset of {1,…,N} of positive density contains a 3-term arithmetic progression.

unknowns.description: a proof of the density ⇒ 3-AP statement

closure.artifact: Roth 1953; bounds sharpened by Kelley–Meka 2023 (quasi-polynomial) and Bloom–Sisask

title: Cap set problem
statement: The maximum size of a subset of F_3^n with no 3-term AP is at most c^n for some c<3 — the finite-field model of Roth's theorem.

unknowns.description: a c^n bound with c<3

closure.artifact: Croot–Lev–Pach / Ellenberg–Gijswijt 2016 — ≤ 2.756^n via the polynomial (slice-rank) method

title: Triangle removal lemma
statement: If an n-vertex graph has o(n^3) triangles, it can be made triangle-free by deleting o(n^2) edges.

unknowns.description: a proof of the removal bound

closure.artifact: Ruzsa–Szemerédi 1976 — a consequence of the regularity lemma

title: Hadwiger–Nelson problem
statement: Chromatic number of the plane χ(ℝ²): the fewest colors so that no two points at unit distance share a color. Known: 5 ≤ χ ≤ 7.

unknowns.description: the exact chromatic number

criteria.test: exhibit a non-k-colorable finite unit-distance graph (raising the lower bound)

refs: de Grey 2018: χ ≥ 5 via a 1581-vertex unit-distance graph (computer-assisted); Polymath16

title: Off-diagonal Ramsey R(3,k)
statement: The order of magnitude of the Ramsey number R(3,k) is Θ(k²/log k).

unknowns.description: the growth order and constant

criteria.test: matching bounds fixing the order of magnitude

closure.artifact: Kim 1995 (lower) + Shearer (upper); leading constant by Fiz Pontiveros–Griffiths–Morris & Bohman–Keevash

title: Sunflower conjecture (Erdős–Rado)
statement: There is a constant c such that any family of more than c^k sets of size k contains a sunflower with 3 petals.

unknowns.description: a proof of the c^k bound
